item.title: Massive funeral for slain supreme leader enters second day
item.description: The dayslong funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in February by U.S.-Israeli strikes, has been marked by chants for revenge.

item.title: 8 people, including 4 children, injured in July 4th shooting
item.description: A 21-year-old female is in critical condition, and 4 of the injured are juveniles between the ages of 6 and 14, according to he police.

item.title: Trump touts American "golden age", attacks communism in July 4th speech
item.description: In a speech delayed by a two-hour evacuation of the National Mall due to severe weather, President Trump mixed politics with history.

item.title: Weather woes impact July 4th celebrations as National Mall briefly evacuated
item.description: Americans celebrated 250 years of independence this Fourth of July, although severe weather put a damper on the proceedings in several parts of the country.

item.title: Trump speaks after hours-long delay for Freedom 250 event due to severe weather
item.description: President Trump spoke in Washington, D.C., at the "Salute to America" Freedom 250 event on the Fourth of July. He highlighted veterans, the Artemis II crew, an early version of the American flag and more in his speech.

item.title: CBS' "The Great American Block Party 250" July 4 special
item.description: CBS hosts a primetime special celebrating America's 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, with exclusive performances and the largest fireworks show in history.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce were married as they celebrated their wedding with hundreds of guests Friday at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Punch Brothers perform "Song of the Water Kelpie (unsung)"
item.description: Bluegrass band Punch Brothers was formed by mandolinist Chris Thile in 2006. Their upcoming all-instrumental album, "The Unsung Adventures of Punch Brothers," will be released on July 24. Here's Punch Brothers performing "Song of the Water Kelpie (unsung)."

item.title: Rep. Nancy Pelosi's husband suspected in Napa County hit-and-run
item.description: Paul Pelosi, the husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, is suspected of crashing into a parked car and driving away from the scene Friday afternoon in Northern California.

item.title: Heat and severe storms damper July 4th celebrations, leave thousands without power
item.description: While millions of Americans across the Northeast experienced record-setting temperatures, thunderstorms in the Midwest downed trees, ruptured power lines and made transportation treacherous.

item.title: Relay for America runs flag from San Francisco to D.C. in message of unity
item.description: Relay for America ran the American flag from San Francisco to Washington, D.C., this week to commemorate America's 250th birthday. They did it nonstop for 20 days and nights for more than 3,000 miles on a mission of unity heading to the Fourth of July. Tony Dokoupil has the story.

item.title: What we know about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding so far
item.description: The vows have been exchanged, the party is over, and slowly, details are emerging about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Some turn to drone shows on 4th of July as a cleaner, quieter alternative
item.description: While many in the U.S. light fireworks on the Fourth of July, some towns are turning to drones as a cleaner, quieter alternative. Mark Strassmann gets a preview in Texas.

item.title: Trump delivers ominous warning to political opponents at America 250 event
item.description: On Saturday, President Trump is slated to speak on the National Mall, but on Friday, he gave a speech at Mount Rushmore that jumped from praise for America to a warning about what he called a threat to its future. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: July 4th military flyover display halts D.C. airport operations
item.description: It's hot and pricey out there in much of the U.S. this July Fourth weekend, but it's not stopping people from traveling. 72 million are expected to be on the move, a new record, with some potential new headaches. Kris Van Cleave has the details.

item.title: A look at the Great American State Fair, complete with a rodeo, flyovers and food
item.description: There's no one way to celebrate American independence, but in Washington, D.C., attendees from across the country visited the Great American State Fair. Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Life-threatening weather amid July 4th celebrations
item.description: Millions are out celebrating America's 250th birthday with events planned from coast to coast. But in many places, including in Washington, D.C., they are also battling heat and storms. Lana Zak reports, and Rob Marciano has the forecast.

item.title: Secret Service special agent in charge on America 250 security: "I think we have a great plan"
item.description: The security in Washington, D.C., for America 250 events involves miles of fencing, TSA-style airport screenings, bag checks, metal detectors and a long list of prohibited items. Nicole Sganga has more details.

item.title: Washington, Hamilton, Adams and other Founding Fathers partied at this bar, still open in NYC
item.description: Fraunces Tavern in New York City, founded before the Declaration of Independence, hosted patrons celebrating the Fourth of July and America 250.

item.title: Eye Opener: America celebrates 250th birthday as Swift-Kelce wedding grips NYC
item.description: Celebrations across the U.S. commemorate the Fourth of July and America's 250th birthday. Meanwhile, Swifties are thrilled by the nuptials of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Pope Leo spends July 4th at migrant cemetery in Italy, meets with U.S. ambassador
item.description: After the pope's visit to the Sicilian island of Lampedusa, the U.S. Embassy said the ambassador to the Holy See gave Leo a commemorative baseball, an apple pie and a U.S. World Cup jersey.

item.title: 7/4: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Holiday celebrations are underway despite a dangerous heat wave; high security is in place for Fourth of July events.

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Punch Brothers perform "New Bike"
item.description: Bluegrass band Punch Brothers was formed by mandolinist Chris Thile in 2006. Their upcoming all-instrumental album, "The Unsung Adventures of Punch Brothers," will be released on July 24. Here's Punch Brothers performing "New Bike."

item.title: Saturday Sessions: Punch Brothers perform "This Land Is Your Land"
item.description: Bluegrass band Punch Brothers was formed by mandolinist Chris Thile in 2006. In celebration of the Fourth of July, here's Punch Brothers performing their cover of Woody Guthrie's classic song, "This Land Is Your Land."

item.title: Serena Williams withdraws from Wimbledon doubles match with sister Venus
item.description: Serena Williams cited a knee injury behind her decision to withdraw from a doubles match at Wimbledon.

item.title: 6 presidential speeches that shaped American history
item.description: These six presidential speeches are some that have most reverberated through the ages, and whose impacts are still felt today.

item.title: Trump announces pardons for pollution violators prosecuted for "fixing their car"
item.description: CBS News previously reported President Trump was weighing pardons of a slate of people convicted of emissions and clean air-related violations.

item.title: More than 100,000 fireworks recalled ahead of July 4
item.description: Federal safety regulators are urging consumers to stop using the recalled fireworks and return them for a full refund.

item.title: Here's who won — and lost — under Trump's "big, beautiful bill"
item.description: A year after President Trump signed the sweeping tax and spending package, its effects on households, businesses and federal programs are increasingly evident.
